{\n\t\t\tif lbl.Source == labels.LabelSourceCIDR {\n\t\t\t\t// Reverse the transformation done in labels.maskedIPToLabel()\n\t\t\t\t// as ':' is not allowed within a k8s label, colons are represented\n\t\t\t\t// with '-'.\n\t\t\t\tcidr := strings.ReplaceAll(lbl.Key, \"-\", \":\")\n\t\t\t\t_, netIP, err := net.ParseCIDR(cidr)\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tif ms, _ := netIP.Mask.Size(); ms > maskSize {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmostSpecificCidr = netIP\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmaskSize = ms\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif mostSpecificCidr != nil {\n\t\t\treturn []*net.IPNet{mostSpecificCidr}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getIdentityCache(ids identity.IdentityMap) scIdentityCache {\n\tidCache := make(map[identity.NumericIdentity]scIdentity, len(ids))\n\tfor nid, lbls := range ids {\n\t\tidCache[nid] = newIdentity(nid, lbls)\n\t}\n\treturn idCache\n}\n\n// userNotification stores the information needed to call\n// IdentitySelectionUpdated callbacks to notify users of selector's\n// identity changes. These are queued to be able to call the callbacks\n// in FIFO order while not holding any locks.\ntype userNotification struct {\n\tuser     CachedSelectionUser\n\tselector CachedSelector\n\tadded    []identity.NumericIdentity\n\tdeleted  []identity.NumericIdentity\n\twg       *sync.WaitGroup\n}\n\n// SelectorCache caches identities, identity selectors, and the\n// subsets of identities each selector selects.\ntype SelectorCache struct {\n\tmutex lock.RWMutex\n\n\t// idCache contains all known identities as informed by the\n\t// kv-store and the local identity facility via our\n\t// UpdateIdentities() function.\n\tidCache scIdentityCache\n\n\t// map key is the string representation of the selector being cached.\n\tselectors map[string]*identitySelector\n\n\tlocalIdentityNotifier identityNotifier\n\n\t// userCond is a condition variable for receiving signals\n\t// about addition of new elements in userNotes\n\tuserCond *sync.Cond\n\t// userMutex protects userNotes and is linked to userCond\n\tuserMutex lock.Mutex\n\t// userNotes holds a FIFO list of user notifications to be made\n\tuserNotes []userNotification\n\n\t// used to lazily start the handler for user notifications.\n\tstartNotificationsHandlerOnce sync.Once\n}\n\n// GetModel returns the API model of the SelectorCache.\nfunc (sc *SelectorCache) GetModel() models.SelectorCache {\n\tsc.mutex.RLock()\n\tdefer sc.mutex.RUnlock()\n\n\tselCacheMdl := make(models.SelectorCache, 0, len(sc.selectors))\n\n\tfor selector, idSel := range sc.selectors {\n\t\tselections := idSel.GetSelections()\n\t\tids := make([]int64, 0, len(selections))\n\t\tfor i := range selections {\n\t\t\tids = append(ids, int64(selections[i]))\n\t\t}\n\t\tselMdl := &models.SelectorIdentityMapping{\n\t\t\tSelector:   selector,\n\t\t\tIdentities: ids,\n\t\t\tUsers:      int64(idSel.numUsers()),\n\t\t\tLabels:     labelArrayToModel(idSel.GetMetadataLabels()),\n\t\t}\n\t\tselCacheMdl = append(selCacheMdl, selMdl)\n\t}\n\n\treturn selCacheMdl\n}\n\nfunc labelArrayToModel(arr labels.LabelArray) models.LabelArray {\n\tlbls := make(models.LabelArray, 0, len(arr))\n\tfor _, l := range arr {\n\t\tlbls = append(lbls, &models.Label{\n\t\t\tKey:    l.Key,\n\t\t\tValue:  l.Value,\n\t\t\tSource: {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_0192eTxVPe8GvkVSbhkZeM8j","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018bzt7w1kwFgngAk31Bz6Lj","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go","content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tlogger := log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\")\n\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger:   logger,\n\t\tlogLevel: logLevel,\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\n\t\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy-audit\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn pal\n}\n\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\t\"policy\":           policy,\n\t\t\"verdict\":          verdict,\n\t\t\"reason\":           reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tswitch verdict {\n\t\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\t\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-decision\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tpolicyKey string,\n\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\n\tresult bool,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\n\t\t\"result\":            result,\n\t}\n\n\t// Add selector cache info if available\n\tif selectorCache != nil {\n\t\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\n\t\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\n\t\tfields[\"selectors_count\"] = len(model)\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tif result {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-evaluation\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tpal.logLevel = level\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\treturn pal.logLevel\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":13,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1250,"cache_read_input_tokens":46835,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1250},"output_tokens":7,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b","uuid":"5d4712b0-86b3-4fac-8342-b3b5afc2dea1"}

(\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\"\n)\n\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"test-policy\", \"allow\", \"matches selector\")\n\n\t// Verify audit log auditEntry[\"identity\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"test-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"allow\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"matches selector\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"deny\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"denied by rule\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"test-policy-key\"\n\n\t// Create a minimal SelectorCache for testing\n\trepo {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"# Simplify: Code Review and Cleanup\n\nReview all changed files for reuse, quality, and efficiency. Fix any issues found.\n\n## Phase 1: Identify Changes\n\nRun `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD` if there are staged changes) to see what changed. If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.\n\n## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel\n\nUse the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.\n\n### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review\n\nFor each change:\n\n1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Use Grep to find similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase — common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.\n2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.\n3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** — hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.\n\n### Agent 2: Code Quality Review\n\nReview the same changes for hacky patterns:\n\n1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls\n2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones\n3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction\n4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries\n5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase\n\n### Agent 3: Efficiency Review\n\nReview the same changes for efficiency:\n\n1. **Unnecessary work**: redundant computations, repeated file reads, duplicate network/API calls, N+1 patterns\n2. **Missed concurrency**: independent operations run sequentially when they could run in parallel\n3. **Hot-path bloat**: new blocking work added to startup or per-request/per-render hot paths\n4. **Unnecessary existence checks**: pre-checking file/resource existence before operating (TOCTOU anti-pattern) — operate directly and handle the error\n5. **Memory**: unbounded data structures, missing cleanup, event listener leaks\n6. **Overly broad operations**: reading entire files when only a portion is needed, loading all items when filtering for one\n\n## Phase 3: Fix Issues\n\nWait for all three agents to complete. Aggregate their findings and fix each issue directly. If a finding is a false positive or not worth addressing, note it and move on — do not argue with the finding, just skip it.\n\nWhen done, briefly summarize what was fixed (or confirm the code was already clean).\n\n\n## Additional Focus\n\naudit_logger.go audit_logger_test.go"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b","uuid":"6c60e054-3cbe-40cc-b780-d49d71c5d885","isSynthetic":true}

83→\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-decision\")\n    84→\t}\n    85→}\n    86→\n    87→// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n    88→// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\n    89→func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n    90→\tctx context.Context,\n    91→\tpolicyKey string,\n    92→\tselectorCache SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tlogger := log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\")\n\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger:   logger,\n\t\tlogLevel: logLevel,\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\n\t\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy-audit\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn pal\n}\n\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\t\"policy\":           policy,\n\t\t\"verdict\":          verdict,\n\t\t\"reason\":           reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tswitch verdict {\n\t\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\t\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-decision\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tpolicyKey string,\n\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\n\tresult bool,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\n\t\t\"result\":            result,\n\t}\n\n\t// Add selector cache info if available\n\tif selectorCache != nil {\n\t\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\n\t\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\n\t\tfields[\"selectors_count\"] = len(model)\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tif result {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-evaluation\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tpal.logLevel = level\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\treturn pal.logLevel\n}\n","numLines":143,"startLine":1,"totalLines":143}}}

verdict,\n\t\t\"reason\":           reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tswitch verdict,\n\t\t\"reason\":           reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tswitch verdict {\n\t\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\t\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tlogger := log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\")\n\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger:   logger,\n\t\tlogLevel: logLevel,\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\n\t\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy-audit\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn pal\n}\n\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\t\"policy\":           policy,\n\t\t\"verdict\": *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n)\n\n// setupAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with a buffer for testing.\nfunc setupAuditLogger(t *testing.T, level logrus.Level) (*bytes.Buffer, *PolicyAuditLogger) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(level, buf)\n\treturn buf, logger\n}\n\n// parseAuditLog parses a JSON audit log entry from the buffer output.\nfunc parseAuditLog(t *testing.T, logOutput string) map[string]interface{} {\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\treturn auditEntry\n}\n\nfunc TestNewPolicyAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test without audit output\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.DebugLevel, nil)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\n\t// Test with audit output\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\t// Verify output buffer is empty at start\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"\", buf.String())\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecision(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"test-policy\", \"allow\", \"matches selector\")\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, float64(1001), auditEntry[\"identity\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"test-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"allow\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"matches selector\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecisionDeny(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(2002)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"restrictive-policy\", \"deny\", \"denied by rule\")\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, float64(2002), auditEntry[\"identity\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"restrictive-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"deny\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"denied by rule\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"test-policy-key\"\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, true)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n\trequire.Equal(t, true, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluationFailure(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"failing-policy\"\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, false)\n\n\tlogOutput := auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n\trequire.Equal(t, false, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogLevelManagement(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.WarnLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.WarnLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestGetLogLevelWithoutAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.ErrorLevel, nil)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestConcurrentLogging(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\twg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo func(index int) {\n\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n\t\t\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(3000 + uint32(index))\n\t\t\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"concurrent-policy\", \"allow\", \"test\")\n\t\t}(i)\n\t}\n\twg.Wait()\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tlines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 10, len(lines))\n\n\t// Verify all entries are policy-decision messages\n\tfor _, line := range lines {\n\t\tentry
